channel.title: Han Zhang | Sociology
channel.description: Recent content on Han Zhang | Sociology

item.description: I am a PhD Candidate in the Department of Sociology of Princeton University. My advisor is Matthew Salganik. I obtained my B.S. in Computer Science and B.A. in History, 2013, from Peking University. My research spans computational social science, social movements, and quantitative methods. My dissertation identifies, describes and explains political protests in China using social media data. Datasets about protest events in authoritarian have been scant. In the first chapter of my dissertation, I use the state-of-art deep learning methods with both image and text data to identify 136,330 offline collective action events in China from 2011 - 2017 from 9.
